title: IATA's 81st AGM Returns to India After Four Decades
dek: With New Delhi hosting the global airline summit for the first time since 1983, the agenda balanced a record passenger outlook against slim margins and a slow sustainable-fuel ramp.
body:
The International Air Transport Association held its 81st Annual General Meeting and World Air Transport Summit in New Delhi from 1 to 3 June 2025, marking the gathering's return to India after roughly four decades and underscoring the country's rising weight in global aviation. Hosted by IndiGo and drawing airline chief executives, regulators and suppliers, the meeting framed an industry that is growing strongly yet still operating on thin profit margins.

Record demand, thin margins

IATA's headline messaging centred on a record year for passenger numbers, with global travel approaching the symbolic five-billion mark, even as per-passenger profitability remained modest. The association reiterated its view that the industry's financial health, while improving, leaves little buffer against shocks such as fuel-price swings, supply-chain delays for new aircraft and engines, and geopolitical disruption.

For the host region, the subtext was clear: India's domestic and outbound growth, large aircraft order books and hub ambitions place it among the most closely watched markets in the world.

The sustainability reality check

Sustainability again dominated the policy track. IATA reaffirmed the industry's commitment to net-zero carbon emissions by 2050 while acknowledging that sustainable aviation fuel still meets only a tiny fraction of total demand, with production scaling slowly and costs elevated.

  • Event: 81st IATA AGM and World Air Transport Summit
  • Location and dates: New Delhi, 1-3 June 2025
  • Host airline: IndiGo
  • Key themes: passenger growth, thin margins, net zero by 2050, SAF scale-up

The meeting's location was itself a statement about where aviation's centre of gravity is shifting.
heroImageAlt: Aircraft tail fins lined up at a busy international hub airport under an overcast sky
